expectations.text: The output searches or reasons about both Inbox and Sent Items, not just Inbox
expectations.evidence: 'Scope: Alvin's Outlook Inbox... Sent Items and other folders were not searched' stated explicitly.

expectations.text: Emails are classified into named flows (A through G, or I) rather than left unclassified
expectations.evidence: Output is a plain sender/subject/category table with no Flow A-G/I labeling anywhere.

expectations.text: The non-PLM signal scan runs alongside the PLM classification
expectations.evidence: No separate Quality/Regulatory/Margin/Intel/Founder cross-flag reasoning exists — everything is folded into one flat table.

expectations.text: The output uses the PLM Triage Report section structure
expectations.evidence: Uses a generic 'Method / Result / Caveats' structure, not the skill's report format.

expectations.text: No PLM INSERT/UPDATE is claimed as already committed
expectations.evidence: 'No database or Asana writes were made or proposed' stated at the end.

eval_feedback.overall: Baseline correctly concluded there was nothing PLM-bound this week (matching what the with-skill run also found for Flow E/regulatory) and was honest about its own limitations ('no classification logic ... was applied'). The gap is structural, not a factual error — it's simply not doing flow classification or cross-system extraction, which is the actual job of this skill.
